# /lcs-deploy

> Build the deployable package from the feature's `output/` AOT artefacts and push it to LCS (Lifecycle Services). The `alm` agent records the LCS asset id on the corresponding work-item.

## Usage

```
/lcs-deploy [--feature <slug>] [--project <name>] [--env <env-name>] [--dry-run]
```

- `--env` resolves to a `project.config.yaml fo.lcs.environments.<env>` entry (UAT / TEST / PROD / etc.).
- `--dry-run` builds the package locally without pushing to LCS.

## Inputs

- `projects/{p}/d365-fo/features/{f}/output/**` (the AOT objects)
- `project.config.yaml`:
  - `fo.lcs.projectId`
  - `fo.lcs.environments.{env}.assetLibraryId`
  - `fo.lcs.environments.{env}.applyAfterDeploy`
- `projects/{p}/d365-fo/features/{f}/blueprint.md` (deployable-package layout)

## Execution flow

1. Validate feature `IMPLEMENTED` (or `DOCUMENTED`) in `currentStates`.
2. Resolve LCS env from `--env` + project.config.
3. Build deployable package:
   - Compile X++ source (via `xpp.exe` or `D365FO.Build`)
   - Run X++ best-practice checks; fail if BLOCKER findings
   - Package via `ModelUtil.exe -create -compress`
4. (Unless `--dry-run`) Push to LCS via the LCS REST API:
   - POST `/api/Assets/upload`
   - Tag with `feature-id` + `project` + ALM work-item id
   - Record asset id
5. Emit `output/_lcs-deploy.log.md` with the asset id, env, ts, and the related work-item link.

## Output

- `output/_packages/{feature}-{ts}.axmodelfile` (always)
- `output/_lcs-deploy.log.md`
- LCS asset id (logged to console + workflow history)
